note: PHASE 3 — SLINGSHOT announced and bumped to activation block 7,000 (alongside Phase 1). Large flashing red 'MINERS & NODE OPERATORS — RESTART NOW' banner added at top of the V11 hard-fork box. Slingshot rule: if previous block elapsed > 30 min, the next block's bitsQ is reduced by 12.5% (single-shot, this block only). The block after returns to normal avg288-derived bitsQ — NO accumulation, NO ratcheting, NO continuous escalation. Self-resetting per block. Activation height V11_SLINGSHOT_HEIGHT = 7,000, gated alongside Phase 1 (cASERT cascade + state-dependent dataset). Operators MUST pull origin/main, rebuild, and restart node + miner before block 7,000 — otherwise their blocks will be rejected by the network after the fork (consensus-critical bitsQ rule). Code implementation is in flight; this commit is the public announcement to give operators the maximum runway. Block 6,989 (129m 35s tail on 2026-05-03) is the case study: under Slingshot, block 6,990 would have entered with bitsQ already 12.5% lower instead of the actual -0.29% avg288 adjustment, making the next nonce materially easier. If 6,990 mined fast (which it did), 6,991 returned to normal avg288 — the relief was only for the one block following the abnormally slow predecessor. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
